question: From the information given below, find which of the point is between the other two. If the points are not collinear, state so. d(P, R) = 7, d(P, Q) = 10, d(Q, R) = 3
answer:
We have,
d(P, R) = 7; d(P, Q) = 10; d(Q, R) = 3.
Now,
d(P, R) + d(Q, R) = 7 + 3 
‚ąī d(P, R) + d(Q, R) = 10
‚ąī d(P, Q) = d(P, R) + d(Q, R)
Hence, the points P, R and Q are collinear.
The point R is between P and Q i.e., P-R-Q.
subject: Geometry Mathematics 2

question: From the information given below, find which of the point is between the other two. If the points are not collinear, state so. d(R, S) = 8, d(S, T) = 6, d(R, T) = 4
answer:
We have,
d(R, S) = 8; d(S, T) = 6; d(R, T) = 4
Now,
8 + 6 = 14, so 8 + 6 ‚Č† 4;
6 + 4 = 10, so 6 + 4 ‚Č† 8
and 8 + 4 = 12, so 8 + 4 ‚Äč‚Č† 6
Since, the sum of the distances between two pairs of points is not equal to the distance between the third pair of points, so the given points R, S and T are non-collinear.
subject: Geometry Mathematics 2

question: On a Number Line, Points A, B and C Are Such that D(A,C) = 10, D(C,B) = 8 . Find D(A, B) Considering All Possibilities.
answer:
There are only two possibilities.
Case 1 : When point C is between the points A and B.

We have, 
d(A, C) = 10; d(C, B) = 8
Now, d(A, B) = d(A, C) + d(C, B)
= 10 + 8
‚ąī d(A, B) = 18
Case 2 : When point B is between the points A and C.

We have,
d(A, C) = 10; d(C, B) = 8
Now, d(A, C) = d(A, B) + d(B, C)
So, d(A, B) = d(A, C) ‚ąí d(B, C)
= 10 ‚ąí 8
‚ąī d(A, B) = 2
Therefore, When point B is between the points A and C then d(A, B) = 2
‚ąī d(A, B) = 18 or d(A, B) = 2
subject: Geometry Mathematics 2

question: Points X, Y, Z are collinear such that d(X, Y) = 17, d(Y, Z) = 8, find d(X, Z).

Case I:
Points X, Y, Z are such that X – Y – Z.

‚ąī d(X, Z) = d(X, Y) + d(Y, Z)
‚ąī d(X, Z) = 17 + 8
‚ąī d(X, Z) = 25
Case II: 
Points X, Y, Z are such that X – Z – Y.

‚ąī d(X, Y) = d(X, Z) + d(Z, Y)

‚ąī d(X, Z) = 17 – 8
‚ąī d(X, Z) = 9
‚ąī d(X, Z) = 25 or d(X, Z) = 9.

question: Co-ordinate of point P on a number line is - 7. Find the co-ordinates of points on the number line which are at a distance of 8 units from point P.
answer:
Let point Q be at a distance of 8 units from P and on left side of P
Let point R be at a distance of 8 units from P and on right side of P.

(i) Let the co-ordinate of point Q be x.
Co-ordinate of point P is -7.
Since, point Q is to the left of point P.
‚ąī -7 > x
‚ąī d(P, Q) = -7 - x
‚ąī 8 = -7 - x
‚ąī x = -7 - 8
‚ąī x = -15
(ii) Let the co-ordinate of point R be y.
Co-ordinate of point P is -7.
Since, point R is to the right of point P.
‚ąī y > -7
‚ąī d(P, R) = 7 - (-7)
‚ąī 8 = y + 7
‚ąī 8 - 7 = 7
‚ąī y = 1
‚ąī The co-ordinates of the points at a distance of 8 units from P are -15 and 1.

question: In the given figure, if ray BA || ray DE, ‚ą†C = 50¬į and ‚ą†D = 100¬į. Find the measure of ‚ą†ABC. (Hint: Draw a line passing through point C and parallel to line AB.)

⇒ ‚ą†ABC = 180‚ąė ‚ąí 50‚ąė

question: A transversal EF of line AB and line CD intersects the lines at point P and Q respectively. Ray PR and ray QS are parallel and bisectors ‚ą†BPQ and ‚ą†PQC respectively. Prove that line AB || line CD.

question: Ray PQ and ray PR are perpendicular to each other. Points B and A are in the interior and exterior of ‚ą†QPR respectively. Ray PB and ray PA are perpendicular to each other.
answer:
Since ray PQ ‚üā ray PR, then m‚ą†QPR = 90‚ąė and ray PA ‚üā ray PB, then m‚ą†APB = 90‚ąė.
(i) Two angles, sum of whose measures is 90‚ąė, are called complementary angles.

⇒ m‚ą†APB + m‚ą†QPR 
= 90‚ąė + 90‚ąė 
= 180‚ąė
‚ąī ‚ą†APB and ‚ą†QPR are supplementary angles.
(iii) Angles with the same measures are called congruent angles.
Here, m‚ą†QPR = m‚ą†APB = 90‚ąė
‚ąī ‚ą†APB and ‚ą†QPR are congruent angles.

question: Line AB || line CD || line EF and line QP is their transversal. If y : z = 3 : 7 then find the measure of ‚ą†x.

line AB || line EF ...(Given)

question: In the given figure, line PS is a transversal of parallel line AB and line CD. If Ray QX, ray QY, ray RX, ray RY are angle bisectors, then prove that ‚Ė° QXRY is a rectangle.

Since QX bisects ‚ą†AQR and RY bisects ‚ą†DRQ, then
‚ą†XQR = 1/2‚ą†AQR and ‚ą†YRQ = 1/2‚ą†DRQ 
‚ąī from (1), we get
‚ą†XQR = ‚ą†YRQ
But ‚ą†XQR and ‚ą†YRQ are alternate interior angles formed by the transversal QR with QX and RY respectively.
‚ąī QX || RY ...(Alternate angles test)
Similarly, we have RX || QY. 
Hence, in quadrilateral QXRY, we have QX || RY and RX || QY.
It is known that, a quadrilateral is a parallelogram if its opposite sides are parallel.
‚ąī QXRY is a parallelogram.
Since sum of the interior angles on the same side of transversal is 180‚ąė, then
‚ą†BQR + ‚ą†DRQ = 180‚ąė
⇒ 1/2 ‚ą†BQR + 1/2 ‚ą†DRQ = 90‚ąė ...(2)
Since QY bisects ‚ą†BQR and RY bisects ‚ą†DRQ, then
‚ą†YQR = 1/2‚ą†BQR and ‚ą†YRQ = 1/2‚ą†DRQ
‚ąī from (2), we get
‚ą†YQR + ‚ą†YRQ = 90‚ąė ...(3)
In ‚ąÜQRY, we have
‚ą†YQR + ‚ą†YRQ + ‚ą†QYR = 180‚ąė ...(Angle sum property of triangle)
⇒ 90‚ąė + ‚ą†QYR = 180‚ąė ...[Using (3)]
⇒ ‚ą†QYR = 180‚ąė ‚ąí 90‚ąė 
⇒ ‚ą†QYR = 90‚ąė
Since QXRY is a parallelogram, then
‚ą†QXR = ‚ą†QYR ...(Opposite angles of parallolegram are equal)
⇒ ‚ą†QXR = 90‚ąė ...(‚ąĶ ‚ą†QYR = 90‚ąė)
Since adjacent angles in a parallelogram are supplementary, then
‚ą†QXR + ‚ą†XRY = 180‚ąė
⇒ 90‚ąė + ‚ą†XRY = 180‚ąė ...(‚ąĶ ‚ą†QXR = 90‚ąė)
⇒ ‚ą†XRY = 180‚ąė ‚ąí 90‚ąė 
⇒ ‚ą†XRY = 90‚ąė
Also, ‚ą†XQY = ‚ą†XRY = 90‚ąė ...(Opposite angles of parallolegram are equal)
Thus, QXRY is a parallelogram in which all the interior angles are right angles.
It is known that, a rectangle is a parallolegram in which each angle is a right angle.
Hence, square QXRY is a rectangle.

question: The measures of angles of a triangle are x¬į, (x ‚ąí 20)¬į, (x ‚ąí 40)¬į. Find the measure of each angle.
answer:
The measures of angles of a triangle are x¬į, (x - 20)¬į, (x - 40)¬į respectively.

‚ąī (x ‚ąí 40)¬į = (80¬į ‚ąí 40)¬į = 40¬į
Hence, the measure of each angle is 80¬į, 60¬į and 40¬į respectively.

question: The measure of one of the angles of a triangle is twice the measure of its smallest angle and the measure of the other is thrice the measure of the smallest angle..

‚ąī ‚ą†P = 30¬į
‚ą†Q = 2‚ą†P = 2 √ó 30¬į = 60¬į
‚ą†R = 3‚ą†P = 3 √ó 30¬į = 90¬į
Hence, the measure of each angle is 30¬į, 60¬į and 90¬į respectively.

question: In a parallelogram ‚Ė°ABCD, If ‚ą†A = (3x + 12)¬į, ‚ą†B = (2x - 32)¬į then find the value of x and then find the measures of ‚ą†C and ‚ą†D.
answer:
Given: ‚ą†A = (3x + 12)ňö and ‚ą†B = (2x - 32)ňö
Opposite angles of a parallelogram are equal.

‚ą†A + ‚ą†B + ‚ą†C + ‚ą†D = 360ňö
‚ąī 3x + 12 + 2x - 32 + 3x + 12 + 2x - 32 = 360
‚ąī 10x - 40 = 360
‚ąī 10x = 360 + 40
‚ąī 10x = 400
‚ąī x = 400/10
‚ąī x = 40
‚ąī ‚ą†A = (3x + 12)ňö
⇒ ‚ą†A = 3 √ó 40 +12
⇒ ‚ą†A = 120 + 12
⇒ ‚ą†A = 132ňö

‚ąī ‚ą†B = (2x - 32)ňö

⇒ ‚ą†B = 80 - 32
⇒ ‚ą†B = 48ňö
‚ąī ‚ą†D = 48ňö ...[From (ii)]
Hence, the measure of x is 40.
Also, measures of ‚ą†C and ‚ą†D are 132ňö and 48ňö respectively.

question: Length, breadth and height of a cuboid shape box of medicine is 20 cm, 12 cm and 10 cm respectively. Find the surface area of vertical faces and total surface area of this box.
